goal: Make Foreman production-ready E2E with layered agents (CEO/CTO/eng) and DoDs at every level. CTO subtree owns CEO DoD #9 (iOS / glasses path scaffold not regressed) plus the technical backbone NOT owned by other C-suite siblings: backend non-security business logic (analyse, transcribe, summarise, voice, db, prompts, routes other than /ready), shared zod schemas + FrameSource interface, web client platform code (web/lib/**, web/app/api/**, web/next.config.mjs), and the iOS scaffold. CTO does NOT own: auth/session/validate (CISO), /ready or structured logging (SRE), test/smoke scripts (QA), consent UX (CPO), docs (docs-truth-sync).
summary: CTO engineering subtree: backend business-logic hardening + web platform + iOS scaffold sanity
acceptanceCriteria: CTO DoD: CEO DoD #9 evidenced -- `cd native/ios && xcodegen generate` succeeds on the handoff branch (or, if `xcodegen` is unavailable in CI, the gap is documented in native/ios/README.md and the item is marked satisfied-with-doc-gap in the final handoff).
CTO DoD: backend AI routes (analyse, transcribe, voice, sessions) have error-path tests in `backend/src` that pass via `cd backend && npm test`.
CTO DoD: web platform code (web/lib, web/app/api) builds clean via `npm run build --workspace web` and passes `cd web && npm test`.
CTO DoD: shared `@foreman/shared` builds clean and is consumed unchanged by backend + web.
CTO DoD: no path in the CTO `pathsForbidden` list was modified on the handoff branch (verify via `git diff --name-only main..<handoff-branch>`).

tasks.name: vp-backend-business-logic
tasks.scopedGoal:
Harden the Foreman backend AI routes (analyse, transcribe, voice, sessions, summarise) and supporting business-logic modules WITHOUT touching files owned by the CISO (auth.ts, validate-media.ts, require-session-token.ts, session-token.ts, api-error.ts, assert-production-security.ts, config.ts) or by the SRE (index.ts, /ready, structured logging). Concretely:

1. Audit and harden these route handlers and modules in `backend/src/`:
   - `routes/analyse.ts`, `routes/transcribe.ts`, `routes/voice.ts`, `routes/sessions.ts`, `routes/labels.ts`
   - `analyse.ts`, `parse-coaching.ts`, `transcribe.ts`, `summarise-session.ts`, `stuck-summary.ts`, `voice-advice.ts`, `elevenlabs.ts`, `db/*`, `prompts/*`.
2. Every AI route MUST:
   - Reject oversize JSON / multipart payloads with a structured 413 (or pre-existing api-error shape). Coordinate payload limits with CISO via existing `backend/src/config.ts` constants if they exist; if no shared limit constant exists, add a local sane default (e.g. JSON body 2 MiB, raw image 5 MiB, raw audio 25 MiB) inside the route or a new helper in `backend/src/` (NOT in config.ts) and clearly comment that it is a per-route fallback awaiting a shared limit. If touching `config.ts` is the only way, STOP and leave a comment in the handoff requesting CISO coordination.
   - Have a dedicated try/catch that converts upstream provider errors (Anthropic, OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Supabase) into a clean JSON response (`{ ok: false, error: { code, message } }` or the project's existing api-error shape) instead of leaking provider stack traces.
   - Validate zod-shaped responses from upstream providers and fall back gracefully when parsing fails (current `parse-coaching.ts` is a reference -- mirror that defensive style elsewhere).
3. Add new `*.test.ts` files (use the existing `tsx --test` runner) in `backend/src/` that exercise the error paths for analyse, transcribe, voice, and sessions. Examples of error paths to cover: oversize payload returns 413; malformed JSON returns 400; upstream provider throwing yields a clean JSON 5xx with no stack; zod-invalid model output yields a fallback coaching object instead of crashing. Tests must run under `cd backend && npm test` and pass.
4. Shared types & FrameSource: if you change anything in `shared/src/`, keep it backward-compatible (the web client and the iOS scaffold both consume it).
5. Don't introduce new top-level dependencies unless absolutely required. If you must, pin a version and explain in your handoff.

Branch discipline: commit to the cloud-agent branch already checked out for this task and push. Do not rename or create branches. Do not open a PR.

Final message: handoff per worker prompt structure.
acceptance: All five AI/business routes (analyse, transcribe, voice, sessions, labels) explicitly handle oversize payloads, malformed input, and upstream-provider errors without leaking stack traces.
At least one new error-path test exists for each of analyse, transcribe, voice, and sessions; `cd backend && npm test` passes locally with the new tests included.
Shared @foreman/shared still builds (`npm run build --workspace @foreman/shared`) and existing consumers (backend + web) compile against it.
No file listed in pathsForbidden has been modified on the handoff branch (verify with `git diff --name-only`).
Handoff `## Branch` line records the actual cloud-agent branch used.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `git checkout <vp-backend-business-logic branch>`; `npm install` at repo root.

## Automated
- `npm run build --workspace @foreman/shared` succeeds.
- `npm run build --workspace backend` succeeds.
- `cd backend && npm test` passes; new error-path tests visible in the output (look for `analyse`, `transcribe`, `voice`, `sessions` test names).

## Manual
- `git diff --name-only main..HEAD` includes only files in `pathsAllowed` -- no CISO/SRE-owned files.
- Spot-check each touched route handler has a try/catch wrapping the upstream provider call.

## Gotchas
- Do NOT touch `backend/src/config.ts`, `backend/src/auth.ts`, `backend/src/validate-media.ts`, `backend/src/api-error.ts`. Those are CISO/SRE territory.
- Anthropic/OpenAI/ElevenLabs/Supabase keys may be absent in CI -- guard tests with mocks; do not hit the real network.

tasks.name: verify-vp-backend-business-logic
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the backend AI-route hardening on the target branch. Run `npm install` then `npm run build --workspace @foreman/shared`, `npm run build --workspace backend`, `cd backend && npm test`. Confirm error-path tests exist and pass for analyse, transcribe, voice, and sessions. Verify `git diff --name-only` does not touch any CISO/SRE-owned file (backend/src/index.ts, backend/src/config.ts, backend/src/auth.ts, backend/src/assert-production-security.ts, backend/src/require-session-token.ts, backend/src/session-token.ts, backend/src/validate-media.ts, backend/src/validate-media.test.ts, backend/src/api-error.ts). Use the verifier handoff structure and pick the strongest claim your evidence actually supports.
acceptance: Test suite output captured in the verifier handoff `## Execution` block with pass/fail counts.
Per-acceptance-criterion finding rows in `## Findings`.
`## Verification` is one of unit-test-verified | type-check-only | verifier-blocked | verifier-failed (live-ui-verified is not applicable to a backend-only change without a running stack).

tasks.name: vp-web-platform
tasks.scopedGoal:
Harden the Foreman web (Next.js 14 App Router) platform code WITHOUT touching files owned by sibling C-suite agents. Allowed scope: `web/lib/**`, `web/app/api/**`, `web/next.config.mjs`, `web/tsconfig.json`, `web/package.json`. Forbidden: `web/components/**`, `web/app/page.tsx`, `web/app/layout.tsx`, `web/middleware.ts`, `web/vercel.json`. Concrete deliverables:

1. In each `web/app/api/**/route.ts` proxy handler, ensure:
   - The handler forwards requests to the backend using the same-origin pattern (no client-to-Render URLs). Use existing helpers in `web/lib/api-url.ts` / `web/lib/api-fetch.ts` / `web/lib/proxy-backend.ts` -- if those helpers don't already enforce same-origin proxy semantics, fix them.
   - There is a try/catch returning a structured `Response.json({ ok: false, error: "..." }, { status })` instead of letting unhandled errors render generic Next.js 500 HTML.
   - Timeouts and obvious abuse vectors (huge bodies) are bounded -- prefer `Request.signal` + `AbortController` if the existing code does not already do so.
2. In `web/lib/**`, add or extend a minimal retry helper (exponential backoff, 1 retry on 5xx or network error) used by the analyse / transcribe / sessions fetchers. Do NOT retry POSTs that mutate session state unless the route is idempotent.
3. Make sure the workspace is type-clean: `npm run build --workspace web` must succeed.
4. Add or extend unit tests under `web/lib/*.test.ts` (project already uses `tsx --test`) that exercise the retry helper and at least one proxy/error path -- the test files already exist; add cases. `cd web && npm test` must pass.
5. `web/next.config.mjs`: it currently sets security headers. If you change it, keep the existing X-Frame-Options/X-Content-Type-Options/Referrer-Policy/Permissions-Policy headers intact. You may extend the config (e.g. add an env-based BACKEND_URL guard) but don't relax the existing headers.

Do not touch any consent UX (`web/components/**`, `web/app/page.tsx`, `web/app/layout.tsx`); that's CPO scope. Do not touch `web/middleware.ts` or `web/vercel.json`; those are CISO/SRE scope. Commit to the cloud-agent branch already checked out; push; do not open a PR.
acceptance: Every `web/app/api/**/route.ts` route is wrapped in a try/catch returning a structured JSON error.
Same-origin proxy invariant documented and enforced (no client-side hard-coded backend URLs introduced).
Retry helper exists in `web/lib` and is used by analyse / transcribe / sessions fetchers; it is not applied to non-idempotent operations.
`npm run build --workspace web` succeeds.
`cd web && npm test` passes; at least one new test case targets the retry helper or proxy error path.
No file from pathsForbidden modified on the handoff branch.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `git checkout <vp-web-platform branch>`; `npm install` at repo root.

## Automated
- `npm run build --workspace @foreman/shared` succeeds.
- `npm run build --workspace web` succeeds.
- `cd web && npm test` passes.

## Manual
- `git diff --name-only main..HEAD` is entirely within `pathsAllowed`.
- Grep `web/app/api` for any unhandled `await` outside a try/catch; flag findings.

tasks.name: vp-native-mobile
tasks.scopedGoal:
Confirm the iOS / Meta-glasses scaffold (CEO DoD #9) is not regressed. Concrete steps:

1. Read `native/ios/README.md` and `native/ios/project.yml` end to end.
2. Confirm `project.yml` still references the Meta Wearables DAT SDK at version 0.7+ (currently `from: 0.7.0`). If a newer 0.7.x patch is officially published you may bump it, but DO NOT cross a major version boundary (no 0.8+).
3. If `xcodegen` is available on PATH in the CI/cloud-agent VM, run `cd native/ios && xcodegen generate` and confirm it succeeds. Commit the regenerated `*.xcodeproj` only if there is a meaningful diff -- otherwise leave it as-is.
4. If `xcodegen` is NOT available (most likely in a Linux CI VM), DO NOT attempt to install it. Instead, append a `## Gap: xcodegen not available in CI` section to `native/ios/README.md` that:
   - Documents that `xcodegen generate` could not be executed in the cloud-agent VM (Linux, no Homebrew, no xcodegen binary).
   - Lists the manual smoke steps a human on a Mac must run to satisfy CEO DoD #9: `brew install xcodegen`, `cd native/ios && xcodegen generate`, `open Foreman.xcodeproj`, build for an iPhone 16 Simulator destination.
   - Confirms the DAT SDK reference (`MetaWearablesDAT` package, `from: 0.7.0`) is still present in `project.yml`.
5. Do NOT modify any swift source files unless absolutely required to make `xcodegen` succeed. Mention any such change in your handoff.

Branch: commit to the cloud-agent branch already checked out; push; do not open a PR. Final message uses the worker handoff structure.
acceptance: If `xcodegen` is available, `cd native/ios && xcodegen generate` succeeds and any meaningful regenerated artefacts are committed.
If `xcodegen` is NOT available, `native/ios/README.md` carries a clearly-titled `## Gap: xcodegen not available in CI` section documenting the manual steps a human must run.
`project.yml` still declares the Meta Wearables DAT SDK at `from: 0.7.0` (or a 0.7.x patch).
No file outside `native/ios/**` is modified on the handoff branch.
Handoff `## Branch` line records the actual cloud-agent branch used.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `git checkout <vp-native-mobile branch>`.

## Automated
- `which xcodegen || echo no-xcodegen`.
- If xcodegen is present: `cd native/ios && xcodegen generate` -- expect exit 0.
- `grep -E '(MetaWearablesDAT|from: 0\.7)' native/ios/project.yml` should match.

## Manual
- If xcodegen was absent, inspect `native/ios/README.md` for the `## Gap` section.
- `git diff --name-only main..HEAD` is entirely within `native/ios/**`.

tasks.name: merge-cto-deliverable
tasks.scopedGoal:
Consolidate the three CTO worker branches (`vp-backend-business-logic`, `vp-web-platform`, `vp-native-mobile`) into a single deliverable branch for the CTO subtree. Mechanics:

1. Your cloud-agent VM was cloned from `cursor/cto-engineering-9334` (the CTO planner branch). Start there.
2. Fetch all three upstream worker branches from origin. Their actual branch names come from the upstream `## Branch` lines in the propagated handoffs in `.orchestrate/cto-engineering/handoffs/`. The orchestrate script also writes the resolved branches into `.orchestrate/cto-engineering/state.json`; read whichever you trust more.
3. Merge each upstream branch into the current branch in this order: backend, web, native. Resolve conflicts conservatively -- favour upstream changes within each upstream's own `pathsAllowed`. If a real semantic conflict arises (same file, both sides changed it meaningfully), STOP and emit a `blocked` worker handoff naming the file(s) and a one-paragraph description of the conflict.
4. After the merge, run `npm install` at repo root, then:
   - `npm run build --workspace @foreman/shared`
   - `npm run build --workspace backend`
   - `npm run build --workspace web`
   - `cd backend && npm test`
   - `cd web && npm test`
   All five must pass on the merged branch.
5. Verify `git diff --name-only main..HEAD` does not touch any file in the CTO global pathsForbidden list (auth.ts, validate-media.ts, require-session-token.ts, session-token.ts, api-error.ts, assert-production-security.ts, backend/src/index.ts, backend/src/config.ts, web/components/**, web/app/page.tsx, web/app/layout.tsx, web/middleware.ts, web/vercel.json, scripts/**, render.yaml, vercel.json, .github/**, SECURITY.md, SECURITY_INCIDENT.md, DEPLOY.md, README.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md). If anything in that list shows up in the diff -- either drop the offending merge (revert just the forbidden file from main) and re-test, or block.

Commit each merge with `git merge --no-ff <branch> -m "merge: <branch> into cto-engineering-deliverable"`. Push the resulting branch. Do not open a PR. Final message: worker handoff with the consolidated branch name on the `## Branch` line.
acceptance: All three upstream worker branches merge cleanly into the deliverable branch (or a blocked handoff names the unresolvable conflict).
`npm run build --workspace @foreman/shared`, `npm run build --workspace backend`, `npm run build --workspace web` all succeed on the merged branch.
`cd backend && npm test` and `cd web && npm test` pass on the merged branch.
`git diff --name-only main..HEAD` includes no file from pathsForbidden.
Handoff `## Branch` line is the consolidated deliverable branch name.

tasks.name: verify-cto-engineering
tasks.scopedGoal:
Final verification of the CTO engineering deliverable. On the merged branch from `merge-cto-deliverable`:

1. `npm install` at repo root.
2. `npm run build --workspace @foreman/shared`, `npm run build --workspace backend`, `npm run build --workspace web` -- capture exit codes.
3. `cd backend && npm test` and `cd web && npm test` -- capture pass/fail counts.
4. `git diff --name-only main..HEAD` -- confirm none of the CTO-forbidden paths (auth.ts, validate-media.ts, require-session-token.ts, session-token.ts, api-error.ts, assert-production-security.ts, backend/src/index.ts, backend/src/config.ts, web/components/**, web/app/page.tsx, web/app/layout.tsx, web/middleware.ts, web/vercel.json, scripts/**, render.yaml, vercel.json, .github/**, SECURITY.md, SECURITY_INCIDENT.md, DEPLOY.md, README.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md) show up.
5. If `xcodegen` is on PATH: `cd native/ios && xcodegen generate` and confirm exit 0. Otherwise confirm `native/ios/README.md` documents the gap.
6. Walk the 5 acceptance criteria in `plan.acceptanceCriteria` of the CTO plan and record per-criterion findings.

Use the verifier handoff structure. Pick the strongest claim your evidence supports.
acceptance: Each of the 5 CTO `acceptanceCriteria` bullets has an explicit `[x]` / `[ ]` finding row.
Build/test command outputs captured in `## Execution`.
`## Verification` is one of unit-test-verified | type-check-only | verifier-blocked | verifier-failed.
